history.qwen_observation: The aspectj-maven-plugin:1.9 fails to resolve com.sun:tools:jar on JDK 11 because tools.jar was removed in JDK 11+.
history.qwen_rationale: Insert a POM_PATCH step to force-upgrade aspectj-maven-plugin to 1.14.0, which drops the tools.jar dependency and supports JDK 11+, before the java8_to_java11 step runs under JDK 11.

history.qwen_observation: AspectJ 1.14.0 (AJC 1.9.14) fails to compile on JDK 11 with 'java.lang.String cannot be resolved' due to legacy bootclasspath/tools.jar handling.
history.qwen_rationale: Bumping aspectj-maven-plugin to 1.16.0 (AspectJ 1.9.20) provides native JDK 11+ support and fixes the AJC compiler's inability to locate core JDK types when tools.jar is absent, allowing the java8_to_java11 step to succeed.

history.qwen_observation: AspectJ compiler (ajc) fails on JDK 11 with 'java.lang.String cannot be resolved' due to missing bootclasspath configuration, a known issue with older aspectj-maven-plugin versions on JDK 11+.
history.qwen_rationale: Downgrading aspectj-maven-plugin to 1.14.0 (AspectJ 1.9.19) provides the most stable JDK 11 compatibility. This version correctly resolves the jrt:/ bootclasspath on JDK 11, avoiding the JDT compiler error that occurs with both older (<1.14.0) and newer (1.16.0) plugin versions in this environment.

history.qwen_observation: AspectJ compiler (ajc) fails on JDK 11 with 'java.lang.String cannot be resolved' because aspectj-maven-plugin 1.14.0 (AspectJ 1.9.19) lacks proper JDK 11+ module/bootclasspath support.
history.qwen_rationale: Upgrading aspectj-maven-plugin to 1.16.0 (AspectJ 1.9.23) resolves the JDK 11+ compatibility issue by using a modern ajc that correctly handles the module system and bootclasspath. Forcing aspectjtools to 1.9.23 ensures consistency across the build.

history.observation:
step `upgrade_aspectj_plugin` (jdk=8): BUILD FAILED post-recipe, rc=1
--- first error cluster ---

[ERROR]

[ERROR] Re-run Maven using the -X switch to enable full debug logging.

history.qwen_observation: The AspectJ Maven plugin 1.16.0 requires Java 17 to run, but the step was executed under JDK 8, causing mvn compile to fail immediately.
history.qwen_rationale: Moving the AspectJ plugin version bump to a step that runs under JDK 17 ensures the plugin's runtime requirement is met before mvn compile is invoked. The rest of the chain follows the standard JDK 8 to 21 progression.
